# 3D Kick Lab ‚Äî Copilot Instructions

## TL;DR
3D Kick Lab is a standalone, browser-based, framework-free (vanilla JS + Three.js) physics sandbox for prototyping a **3D ball-flight extension** to [rcssserver](../../rcssserver) (RoboCup Soccer Simulation 2D server). It is NOT part of rcssserver's build ‚Äî it's a sibling directory under `D:\workspace\robo\ss2d` used to visually experiment with kick power/direction/loft, gravity, and bounce before porting any formula back into rcssserver's C++ (`object.cpp`/`player.cpp`).

- Entry point: open [index.html](../index.html) directly in a browser (double-click, or `py -m http.server 8000` and browse to it). No build step, no `npm install`, no bundler.
- Core physics engine: [physics.js](../physics.js) ‚Äî pure, dependency-free, dual-exported for both browser (`window.KickLabPhysics`) and Node (`module.exports`, used by `debug_trace.js`).
- Rendering/UI: [main.js](../main.js) (Three.js scene, playback controls, parameter sliders) + [index.html](../index.html) (markup) + [style.css](../style.css) (dark theme).
- Headless debug tool: [debug_trace.js](../debug_trace.js) ‚Äî run physics traces from the CLI without a browser, writes CSV-style logs to `logs/` (gitignored).

## Architecture
```
physics.js  ‚îÄ‚îÄ(window.KickLabPhysics)‚îÄ‚îÄ>  main.js  ‚îÄ‚îÄrenders‚îÄ‚îÄ>  Three.js scene (index.html canvas)
   ‚îÇ                                         ‚îÇ
   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ(module.exports)‚îÄ‚îÄ> debug_trace.js ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò (both consume the SAME engine, no duplication)
```
- **`physics.js`** owns all simulation state and rules: `class KickLabPhysics` with `reset()`, `kick(power, dirDeg, loftDeg)`, `step()`, `isBallKickable()`, `speed()`. It has **zero DOM/Three.js dependencies** ‚Äî this is intentional so it can run identically in the browser and in Node (`debug_trace.js`) for fast, visual-free regression checks.
- **`main.js`** is the only file that touches Three.js/DOM. It builds the scene (field, goals, player cylinder, ball sphere, trail), wires every UI control (sliders, buttons, coordinate overlays), and drives the `KickLabPhysics` instance frame-by-frame via `requestAnimationFrame`.
- **Coordinate convention**: physics space is `(x, y, z)` where `x,y` = ground plane (matching rcssserver's field coords) and `z` = height (up). Three.js is Y-up, so every physics‚Üírender conversion goes through `physToThree(p) => new THREE.Vector3(p.x, p.z, p.y)`. Never construct a `THREE.Vector3` directly from a physics point without this mapping.
- **Player is always fixed at the origin** `(0,0)`, facing `+X` (rcssserver body-angle convention: 0¬∞ = facing +X). Only the ball moves in the simulation.
- **Field/goal dimensions mirror rcssserver's real `ServerParam` values**: pitch `105 x 68`, goals at `x = ¬±52.5` (i.e. `PITCH_LENGTH/2`), goal width `7.32`, goal height `2.44`. If rcssserver's `serverparam.cpp` constants ever change, update `main.js`'s `fieldW/fieldH/GOAL_X/GOAL_WIDTH/GOAL_HEIGHT` to match.

## Critical Convention: Per-Cycle Integration (NOT real-world SI units)
`physics.js`'s `step()` integrates **per cycle**, exactly like rcssserver's `MPObject::_inc()` (`pos += vel`, `vel *= decay` ‚Äî no `dt` multiplication inside the physics itself). `dt` (default `0.1`) is metadata used only by `main.js` for *playback pacing* (how many sim cycles to advance per wall-clock second), never inside `kick()`/`step()`'s velocity or position math.

**Do not reintroduce `dt` multiplication into the integrator.** A previous bug did exactly this (double-scaling kick velocities that were already per-cycle-scale, combined with real-world `gravity=9.8`), causing high-loft kicks to barely leave the ground. If you touch `step()`, always sanity-check with `debug_trace.js` (see Build & Test) ‚Äî a `loft=60` kick with `power=100` should reach several meters of peak height over ~5-10 cycles, not stay near 0.

## Patterns & Conventions
- **No framework, no bundler, no `node_modules` at runtime.** Three.js r128 and OrbitControls are loaded via CDN `<script>` tags in `index.html` (cdnjs / jsdelivr). Node is used ONLY for `debug_trace.js` and `node --check` syntax validation ‚Äî never assume a browser-only file can `require()`.
- **Dual export guard** at the bottom of `physics.js` ‚Äî any new export must go through both branches:
  ```js
  if (typeof window !== "undefined") { window.KickLabPhysics = KickLabPhysics; window.DEFAULT_PARAMS = DEFAULT_PARAMS; }
  if (typeof module !== "undefined" && module.exports) { module.exports = { KickLabPhysics, DEFAULT_PARAMS }; }
  ```
- **Every tunable constant lives in `DEFAULT_PARAMS`** (physics.js) ‚Äî never hardcode a magic number for a physics constant in `main.js`; add it to `DEFAULT_PARAMS`, wire a slider via the `PARAM_RANGES` dynamic-slider loop in `main.js`, and add an entry to the `DESCRIPTIONS` map so the "!" info button explains it.
- **Every slider gets an info (`!`) button and a reset (`‚Ü∫`) button** via `attachInfoButton(rowEl, key, defaultValue)` in `main.js`. When adding a new physics parameter or kick-command slider, follow this exact pattern (see `PARAM_RANGES.forEach(...)` and the `row_power`/`row_dir`/`row_loft` calls) rather than wiring a bare `<input type=range>`.
- **Rendering objects added on top of the ground (trail, markers) must set `depthTest:false` and a high `renderOrder`** to avoid z-fighting with the field/grid meshes (both sit at `y‚âà0`). See the trail setup in `main.js` for the pattern (`TRAIL_LIFT`, `trailMat`, `trailPointsMat`).
- **DOM-overlay labels, not Three.js sprites**, are used for coordinate readouts (mouse-hover, ball position, axis ticks) ‚Äî positioned via `worldToScreen()` (`vec3.clone().project(camera)`). Follow this pattern for any new on-screen text overlay; it stays crisp at any zoom level, unlike sprite-based text.
- **Label-overlap override sub-pattern**: when a DOM-overlay label's world anchor is colocated with another visual element (so the default centered transform would overlap it), give the label an extra CSS class and override its `transform` there instead of changing the shared base class. `addAxisLabel(physX, physY, text, extraClass)` takes an optional 4th param for exactly this ‚Äî e.g. the `"origin (0,0)"` axis label sits exactly at the player's base, so it's tagged `"originLabel"` and `.originLabel { transform: translate(calc(-100% - 24px), -50%) !important; }` (style.css) pushes it left of the anchor. The ball's coordinate label (`.ballCoord`) uses the same idea directly on its base class (`translate(14px, -190%)`) to sit at the ball's top-right instead of centered above it.
- **Sticky action-button toolbar**: Step/Play/Pause/Reset/Kick live in one shared `#toolbar` div at the very top of `#panel` (index.html), NOT inside their old individual `.section`s. `#toolbar` uses `position: sticky; top: 0;` (style.css) so it stays pinned while the rest of `#panel` scrolls beneath it ‚Äî this only works because `#panel` itself is the nearest scrolling ancestor (`overflow-y: auto`). All 5 buttons are looked up purely by ID in `main.js` (`$("btnX").onclick = ...`), so relocating them in the DOM never requires touching event-wiring code; if you add a new global action button, put it in `#toolbar` rather than a `.section` so it stays visible while scrolling.
- **Boolean toggle parameters use a checkbox, not a range slider**: `PARAM_RANGES`/the range-slider generator loop in `main.js` only handles numeric min/max/step params. Boolean physics params (e.g. `precise_bounce_timing`) go through a separate `BOOL_PARAMS` array + dedicated checkbox-building loop right before the `PARAM_RANGES` loop ‚Äî same "!"/"‚Ü∫" button decoration, but wired via `.checked` instead of `.value`. `resettableInputs` entries for checkboxes carry an `isCheckbox: true` flag so the "Reset ALL parameters" handler knows to set `.checked` instead of `.value`.
- **Client-side persistence goes through `localStorage`, never a real backend.** This is a static, serverless page (see Build & Test ‚Äî no build step, opened directly or via `py -m http.server`), so any "save/compare/persist" feature (e.g. Saved Runs, below) must store its data as JSON in `localStorage` behind a single versioned key (e.g. `kicklab_saved_runs_v1` ‚Äî bump the suffix if you ever change the stored shape in a backward-incompatible way) and read/write through small `loadX()`/`persistX()` wrapper functions that `try/catch` around `JSON.parse`/`localStorage.setItem` (quota errors, corrupted data, private-browsing restrictions must not crash the page).

## Key Abstractions
- **`KickLabPhysics`** (`physics.js`) ‚Äî the whole simulation. Key methods: `reset(overrides)` (re-initializes ball at `init_x/y/z/vx/vy/vz`, player fixed at origin), `kick(power, dirDeg, loftDeg)` (ports rcssserver's `eff_power` formula, splits into horizontal/vertical components via the loft angle, applies `loft_power_cost` penalty; `effPower` is also graduated down by `dirDiff` (angle-off), `distBall` (distance-off), and ‚Äî since the `height_power_cost` feature ‚Äî `heightFrac = ball.pos.z / player_reach_height` via a `- p.height_power_cost * heightFrac` term, so headers/lofted-ball kicks lose power the closer the ball is to the reach-height ceiling; all four terms are surfaced in `lastKickInfo` for the on-screen kick-info readout), `step()` (gravity, position integration, ground bounce w/ `ball_bounce_restitution`, ground/air xy-decay split via `ball_decay`/`air_decay`, plus `roll_stop_speed`/`bounce_stop_speed` settle checks ‚Äî gravity is skipped once the ball is resting on the ground to avoid perpetual micro-bounce, and the post-bounce settle check compares the *predicted* post-bounce velocity, not the incoming fall velocity, against `bounce_stop_speed`; every bounce (in both the precise and legacy timing branches, and in both their "settle" and "keep bouncing" sub-cases) also calls the new `_applyBounceFriction(vzImpact, postBounceVz)` helper, which couples the vertical impulse absorbed at impact to a one-time horizontal (x,y) speed loss via `ball_bounce_friction` ‚Äî see below; also truncates `history` to the current `cycle` before pushing a new snapshot, so scrubbing back via the Step bar then stepping/playing forward again doesn't leave stale "future" entries), `isBallKickable()` (2D circle test AND `pos.z <= player_reach_height` gate ‚Äî a kick beyond reach height cannot be triggered), `isAtRest()` (exact check: `pos.z <= 0` and `vx === vy === vz === 0`; used by `main.js` to auto-stop playback the instant the ball fully settles).
  - **`_applyBounceFriction(vzImpact, postBounceVz)`** (`physics.js`, new): called at every ground bounce right before the reflected `vel.z` is applied. `normalImpulse = |vzImpact| - |postBounceVz|` is the vertical speed the bounce just absorbed; the ball's horizontal speed is scaled down by up to `ball_bounce_friction * normalImpulse` (clamped so it can never go negative or reverse direction) ‚Äî a Coulomb-friction-style coupling inspired by how SimSpark/ODE resolve a bounce as one coupled normal+tangential impulse rather than two independent axes (see `../compare.md`). `ball_bounce_friction = 0` (disable) reproduces the old behavior where a bounce never touches `vx`/`vy` ‚Äî only the continuous `ball_decay`/`air_decay` friction slows horizontal motion over time. Default is `0.3`.
  - **`precise_bounce_timing`** (boolean param, **default `true`**): `step()` finds the exact fractional point within the cycle where `z` would cross `0` (linear interpolation, since one cycle moves the ball at a constant `vel.z`), bounces the velocity at that instant, then continues moving for the *remaining* fraction of the cycle with the reflected velocity (a "mirror" correction) instead of chopping the cycle short ‚Äî this is a continuous/analytical time-of-impact scheme, the same style rcssserver itself already uses for ball-vs-goalpost collision (see `../compare.md`), which is why it was promoted to the default. When `false` (legacy/experimental toggle, kept for A/B comparison), a cycle that would carry the ball below `z=0` instead just clamps `pos.z` straight to `0` and bounces from there, discarding whatever fraction of that cycle's fall happened after the true ground-crossing instant ‚Äî always sanity-check both settings with `debug_trace.js` if you touch this logic.
- **`main.js`'s animate loop** ‚Äî `requestAnimationFrame` loop accumulates wall time √ó speed multiplier, steps the sim in a bounded `while` loop (guard `<50`), calling `sim.isAtRest()` after every `sim.step()` and immediately invoking `stopPlaying()` (then breaking) once true ‚Äî this guarantees Play always halts when the ball settles, regardless of where the Step scrubber currently sits ‚Äî then calls `syncMeshes()`, `updateReadouts()`, `updateAxisLabels()`, `updateMouseCoordLabel()`, `updateBallCoordLabel()`, `renderer.render(...)` in that order every frame.
- **`resettableInputs[]`** ‚Äî array populated by every `attachInfoButton()` call; the "Reset ALL parameters" button iterates this array to restore every slider to its `DEFAULT_PARAMS`/`KICK_DEFAULTS` value.
- **Saved Runs (compare)** (`main.js`, "Saved runs (compare)" `.section` in `index.html`, right after "Kick command") ‚Äî lets you snapshot a completed kick's full trajectory + params, then re-render any number of past runs simultaneously in distinct colors alongside the live simulation, for A/B-style visual comparison.
  - **Storage**: an array of run objects in `localStorage["kicklab_saved_runs_v1"]` via `loadSavedRuns()`/`persistSavedRuns()`. Each run = `{ id, name, savedAt, color, cycle, time, maxHeightReached, trail, params, kick:{power,dir,loft}, init:{x,y,z,vx,vy,vz} }` ‚Äî `trail` is a deep-copied snapshot of `sim.trail` at save time (so a saved run replays its exact recorded path with **no re-simulation**), `params` is a shallow copy of the *entire* live `sim.params` (all `DEFAULT_PARAMS` keys, not just the ones with UI sliders), so "apply" always has the full physics config to restore, not just what's currently exposed in `PARAM_RANGES`/`BOOL_PARAMS`.
  - **`btnSaveRun`** captures the state above into a new run, assigns it the next color from the fixed 10-entry `RUN_COLORS` palette (cycled by index, `RUN_COLORS[savedRuns.length % RUN_COLORS.length]`), pushes it, persists, and re-renders the list.
  - **Compare rendering**: `buildCompareTrail(run)`/`removeCompareTrail(id)` build/tear down a dedicated `THREE.Line` + `THREE.Points` pair per checked run (mirrors the live trail's own `depthTest:false` / high-`renderOrder` pattern above so compare trails never z-fight the field/grid), keyed in a `compareTrails` Map by run id. Checking a run's checkbox in `#savedRunsList` is the ONLY thing that adds/removes its mesh from the scene ‚Äî saved data and "currently displayed" are deliberately decoupled, so unchecking never deletes the run.
  - **`btnUncheckAllRuns`** ("clear screen") removes every currently-rendered compare mesh WITHOUT touching `localStorage` ‚Äî a purely visual reset. **`btnClearAllRuns`** ("clear DB") does the opposite: wipes every saved run from `localStorage` (guarded by a native `confirm()`, since it's irreversible) and clears any rendered meshes too.
  - **View/Apply params**: each list row (`renderSavedRunsList()`) gets a "params" toggle button (shows `fmtParamsBlock(run)` ‚Äî same open/close `.paramDesc`-style pattern as the physics-parameter "!" info buttons) and an "apply" button (`applyRunParams(run)`) that pushes the run's kick sliders + `init_*` fields directly, then **reuses the existing `resettableInputs[]` array** (see above) to drive every matching physics slider/checkbox ‚Äî this is why `applyRunParams` doesn't need its own param-name-to-DOM-element mapping; it piggybacks on the same array the "Reset ALL parameters" button already maintains. If you add a new physics param (per the `DEFAULT_PARAMS`/`PARAM_RANGES`/`BOOL_PARAMS` pattern above), applying a saved run automatically picks it up too ‚Äî no changes needed here.
  - **Do NOT auto re-render on save/apply**: saving or applying a run never touches `sim`/triggers a Reset or Kick automatically ‚Äî the user must still press Reset/Kick themselves after "apply" loads a run's params onto the sliders. Keep this decoupling if you extend this feature.

## Build & Test
- **No build step.** Open `index.html` in a browser, or serve statically: `py -m http.server 8000` from this directory.
- **Syntax check** (fast, no browser needed): `node --check main.js` and `node --check physics.js` ‚Äî run this after ANY edit to either file.
- **Physics regression check**: `node debug_trace.js [power] [dir] [loft] [cycles] [paramOverridesJSON]` ‚Äî headless kick trace, writes `logs/trace_<timestamp>.log` (gitignored) with per-cycle `x,y,z,vx,vy,vz,speed` plus a peak-height summary. Use this to verify any change to `kick()`/`step()` before trusting the visual result ‚Äî e.g. `node debug_trace.js 100 0 60 60` should show a real airborne arc (several meters peak height), not a near-zero hop.
- There is no automated assertion-based test suite ‚Äî `debug_trace.js` output must be inspected manually (peak height / bounce count / settle cycle) against expectations.

## Integration Points
- **rcssserver** ([../../rcssserver](../../rcssserver)) is the reference codebase this tool prototypes against ‚Äî constants and formulas here are ported from `src/serverparam.cpp` (`ServerParam` constants), `src/player.cpp` (`Player::kick()`, `ballKickable()`), and `src/object.cpp` (`MPObject::_inc()` integration convention). When rcssserver's real formulas change, re-check this tool's `physics.js` for drift.
- **GitHub repo**: `git@github.com:rcsoccersim2-5D/3d-kick-lab.git`, branch `main`. `.gitignore` excludes `logs/` and `*.log` ‚Äî never commit debug trace output.
- No CI configured for this project (unlike rcssserver's CircleCI).

## Important Notes
- **Never multiply position/velocity integration by `dt`** inside `physics.js` ‚Äî see "Critical Convention" above.
- **Three.js cylinder rotation axes**: rotating a default-oriented (Y-axis-aligned) cylinder about `Z` maps its axis onto `X`; rotating about `X` maps it onto `Z`. The goal crossbar previously had this backwards (`rotation.z` instead of `rotation.x`) ‚Äî double-check axis choice whenever rotating a primitive to span a specific world axis.
- **`git init` defaults to branch `master`** unless `init.defaultBranch` is configured ‚Äî if you ever need to re-init or troubleshoot pushes, check `git branch` before assuming `main` exists locally.
- **GitHub Pages is enabled** for this repo (Settings ‚Üí Pages ‚Üí Deploy from branch `main`, root) and deploys automatically on every push to `main` via GitHub's own managed Pages deployment (no `.github/workflows/*.yml` in this repo ‚Äî there's no custom Actions workflow to edit; the artifact-build/deploy pipeline is entirely GitHub-managed). If a deploy run fails with `Error: Deployment failed, try again later.`, that is a transient GitHub Pages infrastructure error, not a repo/code problem ‚Äî there is nothing in this repo to "fix" for it. Just push again (or re-run the failed job from the repo's Actions tab); if it repeats, check Settings ‚Üí Pages for an error banner and confirm only one Pages deployment is running at a time (concurrent deployments to the same environment can collide).
- **Bounce-settle check must use the *predicted* post-bounce velocity, not the incoming fall velocity**, when comparing against `bounce_stop_speed` in `step()`. Checking the incoming velocity converges to a stable non-decaying micro-oscillation instead of actually stopping ‚Äî always run `debug_trace.js` after touching the bounce logic and confirm the trace reaches a final resting cycle (e.g. "Ball stopped rolling." at a fixed cycle) rather than oscillating forever.
- **`step()` must truncate `this.history` to `this.cycle` before pushing a new snapshot** (`if (this.history.length > this.cycle) this.history.length = this.cycle;`). Without this, scrubbing backward via the Step bar and then stepping/playing forward again leaves stale "future" history entries in place, desyncing the invariant `history[idx].cycle === idx` that the scrubber relies on.
- **Camera framing convention**: `camera.position` and `controls.target` are aimed further down-field (`+x`) than the player's origin (e.g. `target.set(16, 1, 0)` vs. player at `x=0`) so the origin/player sits toward the left of the viewport rather than dead-center ‚Äî keep this offset in mind if retuning the camera.
